serving.description: 1 plate of chicken bites with cheese sauce
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200 g, comprising a plate of deep-fried breaded chicken bites accompanied by a generous portion of creamy nacho cheese sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, nuggets, breaded, frozen, prepared (SR Legacy 21420) and Cheese sauce, ready-to-serve (SR Legacy 01018); values blended to reflect 75% chicken bites and 25% cheese sauce composition.
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Cheesy Chicken Bites' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values were estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried chicken nuggets (FNDDS 21420, ~75% weight) and ready-to-serve cheese sauce (SR Legacy 01018, ~25% weight). The input record's per_100g values (energy 325 kcal, protein 17.5 g, fat 22.5 g, carbohydrate 12.5 g) are broadly consistent with this blended estimate and have been retained with medium confidence. Sodium at 600 mg/100g is plausible for this combination. All values cross-checked against HPB Singapore Foodo fried chicken category ranges.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous food: chicken nuggets / breaded fried chicken (GI ~65, medium category).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cheesy chicken bites with nacho cheese sauce. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded fried chicken / chicken nuggets (GI ~65, medium). The breadcrumb coating and wheat flour are the primary glycemic drivers; the cheese sauce contributes minimal additional carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 25.0) / 100 = 16.3 (200 g serving × 12.5 g carbohydrate/100g = 25.0 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 600 mg/100g; saturated fat at 7.5 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: At 600 mg sodium per 100 g, a single 200 g serving delivers 1,200 mg sodium — approximately 52% of the WHO daily limit of 2,300 mg — making this food unsuitable for individuals with hypertension; portion should be strictly limited and high-sodium dipping sauces avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined breadcrumb coating with low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100g); trans fat (0.25 g/100g) impairing insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: The refined wheat flour and breadcrumb coating produce a medium GI of 65 with a glycemic load of 16.3 per 200 g serving, and the low fibre content (1.0 g/100g) promotes rapid postprandial glucose rise; trans fat further impairs insulin sensitivity, making this food unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 7.5 g/100g; trans fat 0.25 g/100g; sodium 600 mg/100g; cholesterol 60 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat (7.5 g/100g from cheddar cheese and frying oil), trans fat (0.25 g/100g from deep-frying), and high sodium (600 mg/100g) presents a significant cardiovascular risk profile; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food or limit consumption to very small, infrequent portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat 0.25 g/100g; saturated fat 7.5 g/100g exceeds 5 g/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Deep-fried preparation generates pro-inflammatory AGEs, and the presence of trans fat (0.25 g/100g) and elevated saturated fat (7.5 g/100g) are hard disqualifiers for rheumatoid arthritis management; this food should be avoided by individuals with RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Chicken Bites is a Western-style deep-fried snack dish popular in Singapore's casual dining and food delivery scene. The dish consists of bite-sized pieces of chicken — typically breast or thigh meat — coated in a seasoned breadcrumb and wheat flour batter, then deep-fried until golden and crispy. The chicken bites are served with a generous portion of creamy nacho cheese sauce, which is typically made from processed cheddar cheese, butter, and milk, contributing significant saturated fat and sodium. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 325 kcal, 17.5 g protein, 22.5 g fat (of which 7.5 g is saturated fat and 0.25 g is trans fat), and 12.5 g carbohydrate with only 1.0 g dietary fibre. Sodium content is high at 600 mg/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) based on the breaded fried chicken analogue, with a glycemic load of approximately 16.3 per 200 g serving. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension (high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (medium GI, low fibre, trans fat), cardiovascular disease (high saturated and trans fat, high sodium), and rheumatoid arthritis (AGE formation from deep-frying, trans fat). It is not vegetarian or vegan, contains gluten and dairy allergens, and is classified as ultra-processed. Common allergens include gluten (wheat flour coating), milk (cheese sauce), egg (batter), and soy (frying oil).

confidence_reason: No exact entry for cheesy chicken bites with nacho cheese sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values were estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for breaded fried chicken nuggets and ready-to-serve cheese sauce, and are broadly consistent with the input record's ai_generated values. Medium confidence reflects the estimated composition approach and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this specific deep-fried cheesy chicken bites dish are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2026-04-16; the blended estimate approach does not yield reliable values for these fields. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible): deep-frying destroys residual vitamin C; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central fried chicken entries. All core macros (protein_g 17.5, fat_g 22.5, carbohydrate_g 12.5) are non-zero and sourced from USDA SR Legacy blended estimate. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore food delivery context; not independently verified. Ethnic tags from input ('Coffee', 'Dessert', 'Pasta') appear erroneous for this dish and have been corrected to Western/fried snack classification. Input trans_fat value of 0.25 g/100g retained; input caution note referencing '0.3g/100g' appears to be a rounding discrepancy in the original record and has been corrected to 0.25 g/100g throughout.
